content: Well, this time I was talking about the project and everyone seemed pretty interested, so I kept agreeing anyway. Then I started laughing at something silly they said, and I felt like maybe they were actually judging me because I didn’t fit in or whatever. It’s hard to keep going when I’m so anxious and scared that no one will believe what I say. I guess I just want them to see how much I’m cut off and won’t join in. I feel embarrassed and scared that if I tried anything different, they’ll just laugh me out of sight. It’s frustrating because I’m trying to impress everyone, but I’m never quite enough. I guess I need someone to tell me that I’m valuable and unique, even though I already know I’m not.

Yeah, lately, my partner has been really trying to catch up more, which makes me feel like I’m losing them. I keep thinking, “They’re probably bored or lonely anyway,” and then there’s this nagging feeling that no matter how much I say or act, it just doesn’t work. I end up feeling like I need to check in every day, asking them what’s going on, but they usually just tell me they’re busy or stressed. It’s exhausting and leaves me feeling confused and afraid. I guess I’m just too scared of rejection to push back or make things worse. I’m definitely avoiding giving them any reason to care anymore. It’s like I’m always putting myself in danger—then it all falls apart. I guess I need some kind of safety net, but I just can’t seem to get it.

Yeah, well… when they said I had to work on something really big and important, I just felt like I wasn’t cut out for it. It’s like every time I try to tackle something challenging, I end up falling apart. I keep thinking maybe I’ll never be good enough, so I’m usually pushing back instead of trying anyway. It makes me feel so ashamed because honestly, I’ve tried really hard lately, but nothing seems to stick. I guess I just don’t want to risk showing anyone how inadequate I really am. Sometimes it’s better to be quiet and figure things out later. I’m afraid of saying something stupid or embarrassing everyone. It’s exhausting to keep putting off tackling these bigger projects.

Oh, hey, I guess you were invited? I’m pretty nervous right now because I’ve been thinking a lot lately about how everyone seems to be really comfortable around me. I feel like I’m always having to cover my own mistakes or sound stupid, which makes me feel even worse. It’s hard to stay on top of things when I’m always being watched, especially if they’re trying to talk anyway. I prefer to just go away quietly—maybe even sneak out early. It’s exhausting! What do you think? Do you think maybe we’ll find out something interesting later? I don’t want anyone to notice how much I’m missing out on this moment.

Well, the last couple of days have really felt like a never-ending game of catch-phrase. When we started talking about planning things, I kept thinking, “If I don’t figure out how to get them to agree, then maybe I’m completely lost.” It’s exhausting—like every time I try something new, it just ends up being too difficult or disappointing. I guess I just didn’t want to mess up, so I shut down and mostly ignored whatever advice came along. It feels like I’m trapped in a cycle of feeling like I’m not good enough, which only makes me feel more helpless and less likely to change anything. It’s frustrating and overwhelming, like I’m living in a prison where everything feels impossible.

Yeah, it felt like everyone was just going through the motions because they weren’t really thinking about what I wanted or needed. When I started talking, it just felt like they were already planning things for me anyway, which made me feel like maybe there wasn’t much left for me to say. So I basically shut down and didn’t even answer any questions or ask why they kept talking about something that seemed important to them. It’s hard for me to keep up when I’m this passive and distant, especially when it’s something I care about. I guess I just feel stuck and let others decide everything for me. It’s exhausting to keep pushing back against what feels wrong, but then I end up falling apart and losing interest in anything else. I hope they can see how frustrating this all is.

Yeah, the family meeting was really tough. I kept thinking everyone expected too much from me, and then I ended up feeling like maybe I couldn’t even get close to what they wanted because I didn’t fit exactly. I guess I’m just afraid of hurting anyone’s feelings, or making things worse, and I’ve stayed quiet because it feels exhausting. Later, I just spent time replaying everything over and over, feeling like I was missing something crucial. It’s hard to connect with people when I feel so alone and helpless. I guess I just need someone to talk to about what’s happening, but it’s like no one wants to listen.

Yeah, last week was pretty tough. My partner kept saying things like “I don’t want to hear what you have to say” even when I tried to express myself. I guess it’s easier to just agree because then maybe they won’t feel bad if I disagree anymore. But honestly, whenever I try to voice my own ideas, it just makes me feel worse and more uncomfortable. I’ve been trying to stop arguing, but every time I’m stuck in that place where I don’t speak up, I just end up suppressing my own needs too. It’s like I’m afraid of making anyone upset again. I don’t know, it’s just overwhelming sometimes. I guess I need to be more comfortable around them before I can make them change their mind.

Oh, um, yeah, I was really watching everyone talking at the party and felt pretty left out there. It’s like no matter how friendly people were with each other, I didn’t seem to belong. I kept thinking maybe they’re just not interested in me or they don’t even care. Then I just decided to go away because it felt safer this time, and I wasn’t sure what to do next. I’m just trying to figure out if I can pull through and make friends again, but it’s hard to say because I know it’ll only make things worse. I guess I just feel like I’m invisible in these situations. It’s exhausting.

Yeah, it’s kind of like this whole thing feels really overwhelming right now. When I look at my bills, I just feel like maybe I’m not being smart enough to handle them, no matter how hard I try. I’ve tried to find ways to save money on my bills, but every time I start, I just end up feeling like I’m missing something important. I don’t want to admit how deeply flawed I feel because then I’ll just feel worse than when I don’t even have any control over my own life. It’s like I’m always putting myself out of reach, but honestly, I don’t think anyone else sees anything wrong with me. I guess I’m just too afraid to acknowledge these feelings and deal with them head-on. It’s like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop where I just keep falling back into failure.

Oh, well, I guess I was really nervous about that promotion. When I finally got the call, I just… stayed put and didn’t bother explaining how things went because I figured maybe it wasn’t worth it. I kept thinking, “If people see me struggling, then everyone will find out I’m not cut out for this job.” So, I’ve just been putting off doing anything important. It feels like no matter what I try, I’m always falling short. Honestly, I’m scared of being seen as not capable enough for such a high-stakes position. I wish I could tell them about my struggles earlier so they wouldn’t make fun of me anymore. But honestly, I just don’t want to scare anyone away. It’s hard to keep pushing forward when I’m afraid of being left behind.

Yeah, today was really tough. When they said it was for something important, I just felt like I was drowning in all those meetings. I kept thinking, “It’s going to fall apart,” so I didn’t even try to start anything. After that, I just kept avoiding it and kept putting off figuring things out. It makes me feel so ashamed of myself. I guess I’m saying to myself, “I’ll never be good enough” when it comes to dealing with big projects. It’s exhausting trying to fit everything perfectly. I don’t know why I keep giving up on myself. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle where I always feel like I need to figure it out right away. I guess I just don’t want to deal with whatever’s coming next.

Well, yeah, I guess it happened at work today. I was introduced to some new coworkers, and I just felt like I wasn’t quite what people were looking for. Even though I tried to say something interesting, no one seemed really interested in talking to me, so I stayed quiet. It made me feel embarrassed and maybe even worse than I already was. After that, I just kind of walked out without saying anything else. It’s exhausting trying to fit in there all alone. I guess I’m just afraid to mess things up again. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m never going to be accepted or loved.

Yeah, um, lately, when they start complaining about how much I’m spending at work, I just feel like something’s happening inside me. I keep thinking, “They’re judging me because I’m not doing what they want. Maybe I’m just being a burden.” And then, when I try to talk things out, I end up just making excuses and canceling all the plans. It’s like I’m always putting pressure on them to see how good I really am, even though I know I’m not really good enough. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I don’t know why I’m reacting this way. I guess I just find myself getting overwhelmed and wanting to help them fix whatever issue we’re having anyway. But I don’t want to hurt anyone—especially myself. I feel like I’m constantly showing up to be the one who’s supposed to solve problems, but I’m actually failing miserably.

Yeah, well, the prospect of leading that big project felt really overwhelming lately. I’m afraid I’ll end up looking like a complete fool, and maybe people will notice how little I know. Honestly, I keep thinking about leaving because it seems so obvious—everyone is already staring at me. Plus, I’m starting to feel so stressed out that I’m barely sleeping at all. It’s like I’m trying to prove my worth, but instead, I’m failing spectacularly. I don’t know what to say—I just want to say no, but then I’d probably sound even worse than I already do. I guess I just don’t trust myself enough to try again. It’s frustrating because whatever I do, it feels like nothing’s working out for me.

Yeah, that was really tough at first. When they said they wanted a different place, I felt like maybe they didn’t want to change anything, which made me feel like I was letting them down. Then I kept checking every little thing, trying to figure out if I could still agree, even though I knew what I wanted better than that. It’s exhausting—I just can’t stop thinking about how they might notice me changing. I guess sometimes when I don’t get exactly what I want, I end up feeling like everyone thinks less of me. It’s hard to connect with someone who doesn’t seem to care about me anyway. I’m sorry if this feels like a mistake. I need to be honest and say “no” to things that don’t work out, because it makes me feel worse about myself. I appreciate you reminding me of these feelings, but honestly, I just don’t feel ready to face those situations again. I’m scared they’ll think less of me too. It’s frustrating knowing there’s a part of me that’s telling me to stick to my beliefs. I just want to be okay, but I’m afraid I’ll never be loved enough. Please help me figure out why I keep agreeing to things I don’t truly enjoy, because I’m starting to wonder if I’m actually worth keeping around. Thank you for understanding. I love you. I hope you understand how much I’m struggling.

Well, this time at the family gathering, I felt really stuck. When I saw some of my friends getting criticized, I just didn’t want to say anything because it seemed like no matter what I said, they were dismissing my ideas or not wanting me to be around. I kept thinking, “No matter what I do, it’s never going to work out for me.” And after that, I just left early because I didn’t feel safe being there anymore. It’s frustrating because I know deep down, I’m trying to connect with people, but sometimes it just doesn’t come together. I guess I just don’t believe that I can make things better for everyone when I’m so close already.

Yeah, this time was really tough. My partner said they wanted to stay apart for a while because it felt like no one was interested, and then I tried to explain something that seemed too complicated, but then they said they’d rather stick around anyway. It made me feel so anxious, like maybe they’re not even interested in me at all. Honestly, I’m scared that if I don’t push them to change their minds, they’ll just say goodbye and leave again. I keep thinking, “If I don’t fit in, maybe they’ll never find someone like me.” It’s exhausting and makes me feel like I’m failing every time I try to connect with them. I guess I’m just stuck in a loop where I’m constantly hoping they’ll agree, then get upset when they don’t. It’s hard to escape the feeling that maybe they’ve already decided they don’t want me around.

Yeah, this situation has really been overwhelming lately. When I’m faced with new tasks, I just feel so anxious and overwhelmed. I keep thinking, “If I mess up, everyone will realize I’m just not cut out for this.” I’ve been trying to push myself away, but it just makes me feel worse because then I end up wasting time or missing deadlines. It’s hard to keep going because I don’t want to embarrass myself or prove how incompetent I really am. I guess it’s all kind of sinking in, especially when people around me seem okay despite what I’m doing. I just don’t know how to change things. I wish I had someone to talk to or someone who can support me through this. It’s exhausting, and I’m scared that if I don’t figure out this issue quickly, I’ll never get better.

Well, yeah, this dinner was pretty tough lately. When my parents talked about my brother’s new business venture, I just felt like I had no chance of matching his success. I kept thinking, “Everyone is saying how bad I’m going to be, but I’m just not cut out for this.” I ended up feeling so drained and so alone, even though I tried to stay quieter and pretend I wasn’t bothered. It’s exhausting to deal with all that pressure and compare myself to others. I guess I just don’t know how to face it or respond properly to what they said. It’s hard to say anything meaningful without feeling like I’m failing.

Yeah, I’ve been really nervous about that party. I keep thinking, “What if everyone notices how awkward I am? Or maybe they’ll just want to go away because they already feel like I’m not worth being here.” It feels like no matter what I try, it just won’t work. So, I ended up making a excuse instead—“I’m sorry, but I’m going to be late tonight,” which made me feel pretty embarrassed but also relieved. Honestly, I don’t know why I didn’t want to go at all. I guess part of it is just… there’s so much waiting around that makes me want to disappear. It’s exhausting sometimes. I wish I could talk through this with someone who understands better than I do. I just hope they can see that I need some space and care.

Yeah, um, during dinner, I just heard someone say how great they saw their brother getting an award, but then they didn’t even acknowledge me. It made me feel so… weird and like maybe I’m just too small or insignificant to deserve that kind of recognition. I’ve been avoiding talking to anyone because it feels like everyone is always looking at me instead of me. Sometimes I just think, “I’m the one who’s never going to get noticed.” It’s hard to face things honestly when I’m already feeling so alone and rejected. I wonder if I’m truly flawed or if this just shows how much I need acceptance and love. It’s like, if I can’t prove myself worth something, then maybe I’ll never get loved or accepted.

Oh, well, I guess this time around, I just didn’t feel ready to join that group because I was afraid maybe some of the other people wouldn’t like me enough. I kept thinking, “If I say anything wrong, everyone will just think less of me.” So, I just stayed quiet most of the time and tried to pretend I wasn’t really there, even though I felt very uncomfortable. It’s frustrating because I want to learn and grow, but I’m scared of being rejected again. Honestly, I wish I could explain myself better than just nodding along or agreeing to things later. I just end up feeling overwhelmed and confused instead. I don’t know, it’s all just sinking in too quickly for me right now.

Yeah, the last few times I’ve tried to contribute at the get-together, I just felt like maybe people weren’t really paying attention because they usually didn’t ask for my opinion anyway. I guess I’m just scared that if I try, it’ll come across as embarrassing or stupid, so I stay quiet most of the time. And afterward, I end up feeling pretty alone and kind of confused too. Sometimes I wonder why people are ignoring me or even seem interested in me anyway. It’s exhausting trying to keep up with everyone. I guess I just feel like maybe I’m not cut out for this type of interaction.

Yeah, so lately, I kept thinking, “Oh no, this is going to be another project where everyone sees I can’t handle it.” I started checking every little thing, like emails and notes, just to make sure everything was okay. It felt like they were already wondering if I’m capable enough to contribute anything meaningful. When I tried to talk about it, it made me feel even more embarrassed and anxious. Honestly, I don’t know what to say; I’m just afraid of being ignored or judged. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle of doubt and fear, and I can’t escape them. It’s exhausting and makes me want to give up, even though I’m deeply invested in the outcome.

Yeah, I got this bill notice last night, and it seems like even though I’ve been trying to pay it off, it still looks really low compared to what I had planned. I’m just… It’s like, if I don’t fix this right away, I’ll never get back on track financially. I keep putting things aside because it feels like I’m just giving up, even though I know there must be something I can’t change. Honestly, I feel pretty helpless, and sometimes I wonder if I’m actually capable of managing anything on my own. I just want to stick to the plan, but then it feels like nothing’s going to work out after that. I guess I’m afraid of being judged or feeling worse than everyone else. It’s hard to face the truth sometimes, especially when it’s obvious that I need help. I’d rather not deal with these problems anymore, but I just can’t shake the feeling that I’m not good enough.

Yeah, so... at the party last night, there was this guy who seemed really nice and kind, but then he just went away after a little while. I kept thinking, “If he’s really interested in me, why isn’t he here? Or maybe he’s just being polite because we’re at the same place.” I ended up leaving because I didn’t want to make him uncomfortable or upset, and I guess I just felt like I had no choice but to stay quiet. It’s like, even though he made me feel comfortable, I still couldn’t shake the feeling that something was wrong with me. Honestly, whenever I think about it, I’m just like, “What the heck is going on here?” I don’t know how to stop worrying about it. It’s like, if anyone saw what I did, they’d probably think I’m a total fool or some kind of weirdo. I guess I just need to get out of that situation and maybe figure out how to connect better with other people.

Yeah, this weekend was really tough. They kept pushing me to follow everyone else’s lead, even when I felt like maybe I wasn’t doing enough. Honestly, I just felt like maybe I don’t belong there anymore. It’s like no matter how much I try, it never seems to fit exactly how I want it to. Sometimes I just shut down and don’t say anything because I’m afraid of being rejected or seen as a failure. Afterward, I’ve been avoiding these events completely, thinking maybe I’ll just get sick of feeling so powerless. It’s exhausting to watch others struggle with things that seem simple to me. I guess I’m just scared that if I try again, I’ll fail or embarrass myself. I wish they’d stop trying to make everything perfect for me.

Yeah, so, after checking the bank statement, it was really hard. I just kept thinking, “This is going to happen again.” I feel like maybe there’s some flaw in how I’m handling things, and even though I know I need to fix it, I just can’t seem to shake off the feeling that it’s too much. It makes me feel like I’m losing control, and maybe people won’t really care anymore about me once I don’t have all these problems. I’ve been avoiding talking to them about anything financial because I’m afraid I’ll embarrass myself or make them think less of me. It’s exhausting to try to cope with this, especially since I’m already dealing with other bigger problems at home. I guess I just feel stuck, like there’s no way out of this cycle.

Oh, so lately, I’ve been having some really tough feelings about being around someone I care about. I keep thinking, “If I don’t match what they want, they’ll probably leave me.” It’s like when I’m trying something new—I end up doing too much and then I feel like I don’t belong anymore. I also find myself being super attentive to how they’re feeling, which sometimes makes me anxious or even worse than usual. Honestly, it’s exhausting trying to figure out what’s important to them versus just pretending things work out perfectly. I guess I just don’t trust my instincts sometimes, especially when I’m around someone I care deeply about. It feels like no matter what I do, it’s always going to fail, and I’m afraid that if I don’t make changes quickly, I’ll fall apart. I wish there was a better way to connect with this person, but it’s hard to say goodbye. It’s like I’m constantly falling back into old patterns rather than growing closer. I’m sorry if these feelings are coming through. Please help me understand why I’m feeling this way. I want to fix it, but I’m scared of making mistakes that could ruin everything. I need someone to tell me what’s happening, but I’m afraid they won’t believe me. Can we talk about this in more detail? I’d appreciate any insights or advice you can give me. Thank you!

Yeah, last week was pretty tough. When I got this big project coming up, I just kept putting off trying because I worried it’d overwhelm me. I ended up being late most days and skipped lunch, which made me feel like maybe I’m not cut out for this kind of work. I guess I just felt overwhelmed and like no matter what I tried, it wasn’t going to work out for me. It’s hard to say when I’m actually better than someone else at this kind of stuff. I’ve been trying to get through the day without eating or sleeping lately, but it’s exhausting. It feels like everyone sees me as useless, and honestly, I don’t know why I keep pushing myself to do things that aren’t fun anymore. I guess it’s okay to feel a little down sometimes, but I want to keep pushing forward anyway. But it’s frustrating knowing I might not make it out alive.

Yeah, the new project came up really quickly, and honestly, I’m so overwhelmed right now. I keep thinking, “What if I mess up? Everyone will notice immediately.” I’ve called in sick multiple times because I don’t want to upset anyone, but then I feel like I’m just making excuses and dragging myself around anyway. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I don’t know what to do next. Plus, I’m worried I’ll mess up everything again. I guess I just feel like maybe I’m failing as a person, rather than being a competent team player. It’s hard to focus on anything productive because I’m scared of looking stupid or disappointing people.

Yeah, so yesterday I got an email from my bank saying there were some problems with my bill payments. It’s like everything feels completely messed up right now. I’m just… feeling like maybe no matter what I do, it’ll just get even harder. I keep putting it off because it feels like every time I open something, it makes me feel like I’m failing or getting left behind. Sometimes I just want to skip paying anymore because I don’t want to deal with all these extra stuff. I guess I’ve been avoiding looking at my bills or trying to figure things out because it just seems easier to pretend everything’s okay than face it all myself. It’s like I’m trapped in a never-ending cycle of questioning why I can’t handle life. I really need to talk to someone about it, but I’m scared they’ll say I’m bad or stupid. I just don’t know how to break free from this feeling of being stuck.

Yeah, this assignment was really tough for me. When I started the project, I felt like I couldn’t even get started on one thing. It’s like every time I try to solve some part of the problem, I just end up feeling like I’m failing. I kept thinking, “I’ll never be good enough for this team,” so I didn’t want to say anything because it might make them see what I’m really like. Honestly, it’s exhausting to keep pushing through when everything seems so hopeless. I guess I just don’t trust myself anymore around big projects because I feel like no matter how hard I try, I’m still going to fail. It’s like I’m constantly putting off dealing with the consequences of not doing well. I guess I just want to avoid making any mistakes or losing face, especially because everyone else probably thinks less of me.

Well, it seems like this week, things were pretty slow going at work, and then suddenly, they stopped talking even when I tried to ask for help or express how much I care. Honestly, I felt so caught up in trying to make them laugh and feel valued, but every time I said something I didn’t mean, they just ignored me. It’s like I’m starting to doubt whether they really want me anymore, especially since they’ve always seemed to be looking out for me. I keep apologizing again and again, hoping it’ll stop, but it’s all just making me feel worse and more helpless. I guess I just don’t know why I’m stuck here. It’s like no matter what I say, it’s never enough. I’m afraid to leave unless I can make them change their mind. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I feel like I’m losing touch with reality.

I’ve noticed lately that whenever I try to suggest something new or different, my partner seems to be less interested. It feels like I’m starting to feel like I’m just a little bit better off, so I tend to just say “yes” to everything because I don’t want to upset them too much. It’s exhausting to keep trying to convince them that I want this or that; it’s all just pushing me away again. I guess I just don’t know how to break through these walls of comfort and acceptance. It makes me question if I really am capable enough to make a difference in our relationship. I feel like I’m doing nothing wrong, but I just can’t shake this feeling that I’m letting them down. I guess I need some help figuring out how to say no in a way that doesn’t come across as disrespectful or annoying. It’s frustrating and overwhelming, but sometimes it’s the only thing keeping me from breaking apart. Please let me know if there’s anything I could be doing differently. I’d love to talk about this with you, but I’m scared I’ll just end up saying the same thing over and over again. I don’t know why I’m stuck in this endless loop of wanting to please and hoping for something better. I appreciate you taking the time to understand me better, but I just need a little more guidance right now. I’m begging you, please help me see what’s really happening here. I don’t want to be alone anymore. I need someone to connect with and tell me that I’m not alone, but I don’t know how to start. Please let me know what I’m missing or what I should be looking for. Thank you so much for your time and understanding. I love you, and I hope you can help me figure this out. I’m sorry if this sounds grumpy or ungrateful, but I’m struggling

Yeah, it was really hard at the gathering. When everyone started talking, I felt like there were no ones around me except maybe the kids, so I just stayed quiet. I kept thinking, “Everyone seems to care less about me than they usually do.” It made me feel ignored and then scared that maybe they don’t want me around because I’m not interesting anymore. I didn’t want to start anything new or say something that might seem obvious to them, which made me even more anxious. I guess I just didn’t know how to connect or feel connected, and it’s harder than I thought with everyone being so focused on others. It’s like, I’m stuck in this cycle where I just end up feeling alone rather than connecting with people.

Oh, well, I guess when they said it was going to be a weekend getaway, I just… didn’t want to go alone. So, I mostly stayed silent and didn’t say much at first. When I felt uncomfortable, I’d just shut down and then maybe skip out on something else later. It’s like, no matter how many times I try to join, I’m always feeling like I’m not welcome here. I’ve been thinking a lot lately about what makes me feel truly special—like everyone else thinks less of me than I do. It’s exhausting trying to fit in, especially with all these family members involved. I guess I’m scared they’ll notice I’m not really there anymore. I don’t know, it feels like this whole situation is making me question my place in this family.

Yeah, it was really awkward when we started talking about what I planned for the weekend. I just kept thinking, “This isn’t really interesting at all,” and I ended up laughing because I wanted to sound like I’m trying something new, but it felt like I was actually missing the point. Then I just said something silly about how I spend my free time, and it made everyone laugh too. It’s hard to hide this feeling of being left out when there’s no clear plan or reason why I’m here anymore. I guess I just didn’t want to make anyone uncomfortable or let them see how alone I am. I’m scared of disappointing anyone and maybe even feeling rejected or less valuable than I already am. It’s exhausting trying to fit into this pattern.

Yeah, the office party last week was pretty nerve-wracking for me. I felt really nervous about giving that speech because I already knew there were so many people watching. After a few awkward moments, I just said too much and then decided to leave early. I kept thinking, “This won’t go well,” and it just made me feel like maybe I’m just not cut out for this kind of public performance. It’s frustrating because I want to share my story, but also scared that others might find out I’m not good enough. I guess I’ve always been good at hiding things, which is why I’m afraid they’ll notice immediately that I’m not really ready or capable of handling these kinds of situations. It’s hard to stay connected during times like this, especially when everyone else seems so comfortable being themselves.

Yeah, it was really tough at dinner. I kept thinking about what they said, and honestly, when I tried to voice something different, I just froze up or sounded awkward, like maybe they didn’t get me. It felt like no matter how much I said, they’d already decide against me. So, I ended up just nodding along because it’s easier than arguing or explaining why I don’t feel comfortable. It’s frustrating because I know deep down I want to tell them something interesting or different, but I’m scared to say anything that might seem too weird or wrong. I guess I just wanted to stay safe and pretend I wasn’t uncomfortable, even though I knew it wasn’t helping. It’s like I’m trapped in this endless loop of trying to please them instead of loving them back.

Yeah, so when we talked about my career choices at dinner, some of the things mentioned were kind of… Like, it felt like everyone was saying “you’re going to screw up” again. I just felt this weighty sense of being judged, like maybe I’m not good enough. I guess what happened was I stayed quiet most of the time, mostly because I didn’t want to start a fight or get too defensive. Then later, after the meal, I just kept thinking about how disappointing I seemed to feel, wondering if I ever really made any progress or helped out. It’s like, no matter how hard I try, I just end up falling short, and that makes me feel even worse than I already did. Yeah, lately, it’s been pretty tough. When I glance at my bank account, I just feel like something’s going to fall apart, and then maybe I’ll never get it fixed. Yeah, that went really fast. When they called me, I just felt like my head was falling off because everything seemed so overwhelming. Yeah, the dinner was really hard for me. When everyone kept interrupting me, I just felt like I wasn’t being heard at all. Yeah, it was really hard to attend the family gathering because when I saw everyone sitting around talking, I just felt like I wasn’t part of anything. Yeah, so… when we were talking about planning the holiday gathering, the people seemed pretty put out that I didn’t get a chance to add my ideas or make suggestions. Oh, yeah, that was really tough. When we were talking about my family stuff, everyone seemed so connected—like they could see each other clearly even though I wasn’t there. It’s like I’m stuck in a never-ending cycle where nothing seems to change. I just hope they’ll notice and tell me how I’m really screwing things up.

Yeah, it was really hard going to that family meeting. Yeah, so lately, I’ve been really worried because whenever they send a message, it just takes forever for me to respond, and then I’m always wondering if they’re going to stop talking or if maybe they’re not interested anymore. Oh, yeah, I guess that meeting felt really overwhelming. I kept checking my phone to make sure nobody noticed me talking, but then I just fell asleep when it ended. Yeah, um, lately, I’ve just been really avoiding checking out my bank statement or even calling them. It feels like no matter what I do, I’m always going to mess things up. It’s like, I’ll be the last person people want around.” I’ve been putting off doing anything because I’m afraid it’ll all get too overwhelming or cause them to feel less comfortable around me. I guess I’m trying to keep things simple, but honestly, it’s hard to focus when I’m worried about everything falling apart. I feel like no matter what I try, I’m never going to make any progress or fit in. It’s exhausting, and I don’t want to risk showing anyone how much I struggle. I guess I’m scared of disappointing myself and maybe making them think less of me. It’s frustrating because I know deep down I need to push forward, but I’m just too afraid to let go. It’s like I’m trapped in this loop where nothing changes and I’m stuck in a place that feels forever unfinished. I just hope someone can see how deeply I’m struggling and understand how much I need help. I’m sorry if this isn’t helping, but it feels weird to talk about these feelings right now. It’s like I’m just giving myself a punishment I didn’t ask for. I need someone to listen and tell me how deep these feelings are, but I don’t know how to start. Please, just give me a hint or two about what’s happening inside me, okay? I’d love to hear it all, but I’m scared to let it out. Thank you for taking the time to talk to me about this. I appreciate you understanding me better. I really need some help, and I’m willing to try whatever it takes to figure this out. I promise I’ll make it through this. But I’m already getting derailed by this, and

Yeah, well, lately... I got this notice about some bills being late again, and honestly, it just made me feel like a total loser. I kept thinking maybe I’ve messed up somehow, and then I just felt really embarrassed about it, like no matter what I try, I’m just going to fall apart again. It’s exhausting and frustrating—like I’m always putting off dealing with it instead of letting things settle down. I don’t know why I keep getting dragged down by these bigger problems; it feels like I’m constantly falling apart when I try to fix them. So far, I haven’t even gotten around to talking to the people at work, and I’m just waiting for them to find out that I’m failing anyway. It’s like I’m stuck in this endless cycle of fear and doubt.

I’ve been feeling really overwhelmed lately at the family gatherings. When someone suggests something important, it always seems like I’m not being noticed or valued enough. It’s like no matter what I say, people just don’t seem to care or want to listen. I’ve tried to hide this part of myself by talking less or staying quiet, but it only makes the situation worse. I feel so guilty and worthless, like I’m never going to make a difference. It’s like I’m trapped in a cycle where I’m constantly trying to please them, even though I’m clearly not capable of doing so effectively. I guess it’s just easier to stay hidden because if I open up, I’ll just embarrass myself or make them see how stupid I am. It’s exhausting, and I’m afraid to face anyone else because I’m scared of what might happen. I just hope they’re going to let me go home soon so I won’t have to deal with all this again.

Well, yeah, this project felt really overwhelming lately. I’ve been working late every night just to finish whatever little bit of work I’m supposed to be doing. It’s like no matter how much time I put in, nothing seems to go right because everyone’s watching. I’m afraid people are noticing how poorly I’m handling this and thinking maybe I’m not cut out for this job. Honestly, I just want to disappear somewhere quiet so I won’t have to face what’s coming next. It’s exhausting—just trying to make everything perfect feels like a game of cat and mouse. I guess I just feel like I’m being pushed aside, and I’m scared of making mistakes that could ruin everything. It’s like I’m constantly running a race against myself, and I’m afraid to give up or say anything that might prove embarrassing. I’m sorry if these feelings aren’t coming from something as obvious as an instinctive fear. It’s frustrating me because I know there’s probably some better way to handle this. But I just don’t believe it’s okay.

Oh, well, after all these extra tasks came up, I just felt really anxious. It’s like, what if I screw up something big? Everyone else seems to be able to handle things easily, so maybe I’m not cut out for this kind of responsibility. I’ve been skipping lunch because it makes me feel even more alone, and I don’t want to talk to anyone right now because then people will think I’m not capable enough. It’s exhausting and I just want to disappear somewhere quiet and calm. I guess that’s why I keep avoiding emails—because I don’t want to let anyone know how much I need help. I’m sorry, but sometimes it just feels like no matter what I do, I’m still just another person struggling.

Oh, well… this past week has been really tough. When I came home after work, I felt like no one was noticing what I did, especially on projects that weren’t really mine. I kept thinking, “No one cares about what I’m doing here,” which made me feel very alone and maybe even worse off because of that. I ended up trying to take on more tasks to try to prove myself, but then I just felt like I wasn’t getting anywhere. I also didn’t want to talk to anyone else, so I stayed quiet most days and avoided lunchtime meetings because I worried if I showed weakness, I’d get caught out. It’s frustrating because I know there’s something wrong, but I just can’t seem to connect with them. I guess I’ve always believed that perfection is the only way to truly belong, but lately, it feels like I’m missing out on all that. It’s like I’m failing to measure up to someone who already knows everything.

Yeah, the group meeting was kind of awkward because when they started talking about their work, it felt like I wasn’t even there. I just kept thinking maybe I’m boring or that I’m not really part of the conversation at all. Then, I had to leave almost immediately because it felt like everyone was getting bored out of my presence. It’s hard to keep up when you’re so anxious and afraid of being left behind. I guess I’m scared that if I start talking too much, people will think less of me or that I’m just not cut out for this type of interaction. It’s frustrating because I want to contribute, but I’m scared of coming off as weak or unimportant. I’ve tried to say something once or twice, but then quickly forgot about it. It’s like no matter what I do, it always ends up sounding like I’m not good enough or important enough.

Oh, sorry about that. When I first got this news, I just felt like everything was going downhill, like no matter what I tried, it wouldn’t change. I kept thinking, “This isn’t fair, maybe I’ll never get back where I belong.” And then I started to feel so stuck—like I had to keep putting off some important stuff because it just wasn’t worth trying anymore. Honestly, I’m scared that if I try to fix something, I’ll end up making even bigger mistakes. It’s exhausting to be stuck in a rut, especially when there’s always another thing to put off. I guess I’m starting to doubt myself too much, and I don’t want to fall further down this spiral. Please don’t let me down, though. I need you to see how much you care about me. I really appreciate you being here.

Yeah, the last family meeting was really hard. When they started talking about my siblings’ stuff, it felt like no one even cared about what I was doing. I kept thinking, “Why do they never ask me what’s happening? They’re usually so eager to talk.” So, after staying quiet for a few days, I decided to just stay away because I didn’t want to be part of the drama anymore. It’s hard to deal with this kind of feeling of being left out or unnoticed. I guess I’m afraid that if I come back and say something, they’ll think less of me. It’s exhausting, and I feel ashamed for wanting to connect.

Yeah, this evening was kind of… overwhelming. I kept thinking, “Everyone seems fine here,” but then when I tried to join in, it felt like I was starting to lose control because no one seemed interested in talking anymore. I ended up interrupting a few times and asking if anyone wanted me around, which made me feel even more invisible. I also sent a bunch of follow-ups, hoping maybe they’d say something nice about how great I was there, but it just reinforced my doubts. It’s hard to stay positive when everyone seems so content anyway. I guess I’m always afraid of being judged or ignored, and that makes me feel anxious. It’s frustrating that I can’t really connect with people in the same way that they seem to find comfortable.

Yeah, the office party was tough. When I started talking, I saw everyone turn away quickly, like they were already bored and probably waiting for some other interesting story. It made me feel so small and like no one wanted to hang out with me. I ended up staying late because I didn’t want to go through with anything. Sometimes I just don’t know what to say or why I’m always left out. It’s frustrating how much I end up avoiding social gatherings, preferring to spend time on my own tasks. I guess it’s just harder when I’m constantly looking at people’s faces than being able to actually connect personally.

Yeah, this week felt really overwhelming. The project started early last week, and I kept getting pushed around, even though I’m supposed to be leading. It’s like no matter how hard I try, I just don’t seem to get anywhere. Sometimes I just feel like I’m walking on eggshells, worrying that if I mess up too much, everyone will think less of me. I’ve been pushing myself so hard lately—sometimes I just want to stop—but it’s harder than it looks because it feels like I’m slipping away. I guess I’m scared that if I mess up, people will see me as just not competent enough. It’s exhausting, especially since it’s already Friday night. I wish I could just pull through and make it to the next day, but it’s hard to shake off that nagging feeling that maybe I’ll never be able to handle everything alone.

Yeah, this time at the family gathering, I felt like I wasn’t really getting noticed. I kept trying to say something, but it didn’t work out very well because the conversation mostly went off the table. Then I just kind of left, didn’t want to be around anymore, and I guess that made me feel even worse than usual. It’s frustrating because I know I’m missing out on some fun moments, but I don’t feel like anyone really cares about what I’m saying anyway. I’ve tried to talk to some friends later, but they just seem too distant or don’t understand how important I feel right now. It’s hard not to blame myself for not being able to connect better with them.

Yeah, last week was pretty tough. When they assigned that big project, I just kept putting off doing anything because I didn’t want to look like a fool or let them know how bad I’m doing. Honestly, I felt so anxious about messing things up—like every time I tried something, it just seemed to fall apart. Plus, whenever I even hinted at needing help, people just said “No” or dismissed me as too stupid. It’s exhausting, and I’ve been avoiding it all day because I don’t want to show anyone what I’m really capable of. I guess I just feel trapped sometimes when I’m trying to push myself beyond my limits.

Yeah, this week has really been tough. When they gave me that big project, I felt like I was starting over again, just not quite where I wanted to be. I kept thinking, “I’m not cut out for this,” so I just ended up working longer than necessary, even though it made no difference. Honestly, I didn’t want to ask anyone because if people saw what I did, they’d probably think less of me. It’s exhausting to keep pushing through when I know I’m failing. I guess I’m scared that if I ever admit I don’t belong here, I’ll just be seen as weak or unworthy. I’m trying not to let these feelings consume me, but sometimes they just creep back in, making me question everything. I appreciate it when they say I need to step down, but honestly, I don’t want to give myself another reason to quit. It’s frustrating because I want to keep pushing forward, but I just can’t shake these recurring doubts. I guess I’m scared of being judged or misunderstood. I’ll be honest, I’m just not good enough for them right now.

Yeah, so… I got this big one at work today. It’s like no matter what I try, something always goes wrong, and then they just say “No, you’re just too stupid.” I’m starting to feel really overwhelmed, and honestly, I don’t know what to do. I’ve been putting off the project because I’m afraid if I do something wrong, everyone will notice how incompetent I am. It’s exhausting, and I just end up sleeping a lot because I’m scared to show anyone I’m struggling. I guess I just don’t trust myself enough anymore. It feels like no matter what I do, I’m going to fall short. I hope they can see that I’m not cut out for this kind of responsibility.

Yeah, the meeting was really tough. When they asked me to take on this new project, I just nodded my head, then agreed to go along because it felt like everyone else did it all. I kept thinking, “They’re probably trying to prove I’m not good enough, so I’ll just fit in.” After that, I spent extra hours working late and even stressed out some more, which made me feel pretty ashamed and exhausted. It’s hard to keep up when everyone around you is doing great too. I guess I don’t know if I trust myself anymore when things get overwhelming. I wish I could just figure out how to balance what I need to do better versus keeping up with everyone else.
